description: Phase C private-bet stack validated live: a browser-equivalent flow computes an order commitment (order_commit wasm), places it on-chain (place_order, which now emits an OrderPlaced event), the committee-service indexer reads that event and serves the Merkle membership proof, the encrypt_order Groth16 proof verifies with those real siblings (commitment + orderRoot match on-chain), and the committee threshold-decrypts the ciphertext to the order amount. Openings never leave the client; only the proof + ciphertexts are submitted.
market_state: LIVE (unresolved, far-future expiry) so the frontend can place bets

validated.steps: commitment -> place_order (OrderPlaced) -> indexer /proof -> encrypt_order proof (verifies, commitment+root == on-chain) -> committee decrypt = amount
bugs_caught_live: indexer getEvents topic filter must be [symbol, '*'] to match the 2-topic OrderPlaced event
indexer must fill leaves by on-chain index (not dedup by commitment) or duplicate/identical leaves leave a 0-gap and the tree diverges from chain

known_limitations: in-browser BLS12-381 proving of encrypt_order (~30k constraints) is slow (minutes); the membership binding to the poseidon255/BLS tree forces the curve
indexer scans the last ~9000 ledgers per poll (fresh-pool orders are recent); a long-lived pool needs cursor-paged, persisted indexing
stake amount is public on-chain; side/amount privacy is via the ZK proof, not the stake transfer
notes: Fresh unresolved market/pool deployed 2026-07-02; web/lib/network.ts points at these. Unaudited research prototype, testnet only.
